env_file.precedence: process environment wins over the file; the file is only read to fill variables that are unset

skills_installer.lock_file: skills-lock.json (written in the project directory where the install runs)

exa.name: Exa
exa.best_for: Semantic and neural discovery: find pages by meaning, find-similar to a URL, and entity search for companies, people, research papers, and code.

keys.free_tier: $10 free credits on signup, no credit card required (~1,000 searches); accounts with a payment method on file get an extra $7 in free credits each month.

official_skills.license_note: No LICENSE file upstream — installed onto the user's machine via the vendor's own installer, never redistributed in this repo.

smoke_test.posix: curl -sS -X POST https://api.exa.ai/search -H "x-api-key: $EXA_API_KEY" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"latest developments in battery technology","numResults":1}'
smoke_test.expect: JSON with a results array (one hit); an {"error": ...} body or HTTP 401 means the key is wrong

tavily.name: Tavily
tavily.best_for: All-round agent web search plus first-party extract, crawl, and site-map endpoints; LLM-optimized snippets; the strongest recurring free tier.

keys.key_page: https://app.tavily.com (API Keys section on the dashboard home)
keys.free_tier: 1,000 free API credits every month, recurring, no credit card required. Keys are prefixed tvly-.
keys.keyless_note: tvly search and tvly extract work without any API key under a fair-use cap

official_cli.package: tavily-cli (PyPI — the npm package of the same name is a third-party squatter, never install it)
official_cli.install_posix: uv tool install tavily-cli || pipx install tavily-cli || python3 -m pip install --user tavily-cli
official_cli.install_windows: uv tool install tavily-cli; if (-not $?) { pipx install tavily-cli }; if (-not $?) { python -m pip install --user tavily-cli }
official_cli.update: uv tool upgrade tavily-cli || pipx upgrade tavily-cli || python3 -m pip install --user -U tavily-cli

smoke_test.posix: tvly search "what is retrieval augmented generation" --json | head -c 400
smoke_test.expect: JSON with results; exit code 0. Without a key this still works in keyless fair-use mode.

perplexity.name: Perplexity
perplexity.best_for: Synthesized, citation-grounded answers: the Sonar models search, reason, and answer in one call — best for Q&A, comparative reasoning, and deep-research reports rather than raw result lists.

keys.key_page: https://console.perplexity.ai — create an API Group first, then the API Keys tab (the key is shown only once)
keys.free_tier: No free API credits: prepaid credit billing, a credit card is required at setup but nothing is charged until you buy credits. New accounts are Tier 0 (50 requests/min on Sonar models, 5 requests/min on sonar-deep-research).

perplexity.bundled_skills_note: Perplexity ships no official CLI or skills (their official Claude Code plugin is MCP-only), so this plugin bundles its own curl-based skills over the official Sonar API — see skills/perplexity/.
perplexity.official_mcp: @perplexity-ai/mcp-server (npm), also installable as the official 'perplexity' Claude Code plugin from perplexityai/modelcontextprotocol

smoke_test.posix: curl -sS https://api.perplexity.ai/chat/completions -H "Authorization: Bearer $PERPLEXITY_API_KEY" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"model":"sonar","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"In one sentence: what is CRISPR?"}]}'
smoke_test.expect: JSON with choices[0].message.content and a citations/search_results field; HTTP 401 means the key is wrong

firecrawl.name: Firecrawl
firecrawl.best_for: Fetching and crawling arbitrary URLs into clean LLM-ready markdown: JS rendering, anti-bot handling, site mapping, page interaction, and change monitoring, plus web search.

keys.key_page: https://www.firecrawl.dev/app/api-keys (or let `firecrawl login --browser` provision it without visiting the page)
keys.free_tier: 1,000 free credits per month, recurring, no credit card required (scrape = 1 credit/page, search = 2 per 10 results). There is also a keyless free tier for agents.
keys.keyless_note: search/scrape work keyless at low volume; a key is preferred

official_cli.package: firecrawl-cli (npm — the npm package named 'firecrawl' is the SDK, not the CLI)
official_cli.update: npm install -g firecrawl-cli@latest && firecrawl setup skills --agent claude-code

official_skills.update: npm install -g firecrawl-cli@latest && firecrawl setup skills --agent claude-code
official_skills.license_note: ISC per package.json — installed via the vendor's own `firecrawl setup skills` installer, never redistributed in this repo.

firecrawl.collision_note: Firecrawl's umbrella skill claims ALL web tasks ('replaces WebFetch and WebSearch'). In this multi-provider bundle the websearch routing skill is the arbiter; do not run `firecrawl setup defaults` unless the user wants Firecrawl to own every web operation.

smoke_test.expect: Shows version, 'Authenticated via FIRECRAWL_API_KEY' (or keyless), and remaining credits

valyu.name: Valyu
valyu.best_for: Citation-grounded knowledge work over proprietary, academic, financial, and biomedical corpora: SEC filings, M&A due diligence, arXiv/PubMed/bioRxiv papers, clinical trials, patents, plus async deep-research with file deliverables (CSV/XLSX/PPTX/DOCX/PDF).

keys.key_page: https://platform.valyu.ai (API keys in the dashboard; `valyu login` also stores one for the CLI)
keys.free_tier: $10 in free credits on signup, no credit card required ($20 reported with a work-email signup).

official_cli.install_windows: npm install -g @valyu/cli; if (-not $?) { irm https://get.valyu.ai/install.ps1 | iex }

official_skills.source: @valyu/cli (skill bundled in the npm package; repo mirror valyuAI/skills)

valyu.official_mcp: valyuAI/valyu-mcp-js (repo install; hosted remote MCP also available) — the vendor recommends the CLI + skill over MCP

smoke_test.posix: valyu search web "CRISPR base editing review" --json | head -c 400
smoke_test.expect: JSON results; exit 0. `valyu doctor` diagnoses auth problems.
